title: PUENTES - 211info
text: PUENTES PUENTES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers supportive services to the Latino/a/e and Hispanic communality and non-English speakers seeking healing and recovery. Provides bilingual/bicultural alcohol and drug treatment and mental health services, case management, mentoring, access and referral to housing resources, continuing care when treatment is finished, and if necessary referrals for medical, psychiatric, and holistic. Treatment plans are individually tailored and offered by clinicians and staff that share clients' cultural background and language. Treatment address conditions like depression, anxiety, trauma, and other life stressors. Plans include behavioral health services such as Mental health care Group counseling, individual counseling, outpatient addiction treatment, recovery groups, peer support case management, DUII treatment. Eligibility: Individuals who are: -Latinx (monolingual/bilingual) -Adults (over 18 years of age) in need of mental health counseling, substance abuse and/or DUII treatment Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 546-9975 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 546-9975 FAX (503) 546-9976 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: Private insurance: copays may apply OHP: may qualify for county funds Below Federal Poverty Level: fee scale or county funds may apply DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Services: Community Mental Health Agencies * Hispanic/Latino Community Comprehensive Outpatient Substance Use Disorder Treatment * Hispanic/Latino Community DUI Offender Programs * Hispanic/Latino Community If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
